questions.question: Statement: 'All metals are conductors. Iron is a metal.' Conclusion: 'Iron is a conductor.' Is the conclusion:
questions.explanation: Using syllogism: All metals are conductors + Iron is a metal → Iron is a conductor.

questions.question: Statement: 'Some doctors are honest.' Conclusion: 'All honest people are doctors.' The conclusion is:
questions.explanation: 'Some doctors are honest' doesn't mean all honest people are doctors.

questions.question: Statement: 'No cat is a dog. No dog is white.' Conclusion: 'No cat is white.' The conclusion:
questions.explanation: From the statements, we can't conclude anything about cats being white or not.

questions.question: Statement: 'Reading improves vocabulary.' Conclusion: 'Those with good vocabulary read a lot.' This is:
questions.explanation: The original statement doesn't imply the reverse relationship.

questions.question: Statement: 'All A are B. All B are C.' Conclusion: 'All A are C.' This is:
questions.explanation: This is a valid syllogism. If A→B and B→C, then A→C.

questions.question: Statement: 'Most students pass the exam.' Conclusion: 'No student fails.' This conclusion is:
questions.explanation: 'Most pass' implies some may still fail. The conclusion is false.

questions.question: Statement: 'Exercise keeps people healthy.' Conclusion: 'All healthy people exercise.' The conclusion is:
questions.explanation: Exercise contributes to health, but health can be maintained without exercise too.
